name: fastapi-inertia

metadata.name: fastapi-inertia
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: An implementation of the Inertia protocol for FastAPI.

classes.name: inertia.templating.InertiaExtension
documentation:
Jinja2 extension for Inertia.js that adds the inertia_head and inertia_body tags
to a jinja environment to render the head and body of the Inertia.js page.

functions.name: inertia.utils.lazy
documentation:
Create a lazy property
\:param prop: The property to evaluate, can be a callable or a value
\:return: Lazy property

classes.name: inertia.utils.InertiaJsonEncoder

methods.name: inertia.utils.InertiaJsonEncoder.__init__
documentation:
Constructor
\:param args:
\:param kwargs:

methods.name: inertia.utils.InertiaJsonEncoder.encode
documentation:
Encode the value
Uses the jsonable_encoder from FastAPI to encode the value
\:param value: Value to encode
\:return: Encoded value

Custom JSONEncoder to handle Inertia.js response data
You can extend this class to add custom encoders for your models

Constructor

Call the property

classes.name: inertia.utils.InertiaContext
documentation: The jinja template context to pass to render the html for the first request.

documentation: Configuration class for Inertia

documentation:
Exception handler for InertiaVersionConflictException

\:param exc: InertiaVersionConflictException
\:return: Response

functions.name: inertia.exceptions.inertia_request_validation_exception_handler
documentation:
Exception handler for InertiaValidationException
\:param request: Request

\:return: Response

documentation:
Constructor
\:param url: URL to redirect to
Exception to raise when the Inertia version is stale

documentation:
Create a dependency for Inertia, passing the configuration
\:param [config]: InertiaConfig object
\:return: Dependency

variables.name: inertia.inertia.httpx
documentation: type: ignore

classes.name: inertia.inertia.FlashMessage
documentation: Flash message type

classes.name: inertia.inertia.Inertia

methods.name: inertia.inertia.Inertia.__init__
documentation:
Constructor
\:param request: FastAPI Request object
\:param [config]: InertiaConfig object

methods.name: inertia.inertia.Inertia._partial_keys
documentation:
Get the keys of the partial data
\:return: List of keys

methods.name: inertia.inertia.Inertia._is_inertia_request
documentation:
Check if the request is an Inertia request (requesting JSON)
\:return: True if the request is an Inertia request, False otherwise

methods.name: inertia.inertia.Inertia._is_stale
documentation:
Check if the Inertia request is stale (different from the current version)
\:return: True if the version is stale, False otherwise

methods.name: inertia.inertia.Inertia._is_a_partial_render
documentation:
Check if the request is a partial render
\:return: True if the request is a partial render, False otherwise

methods.name: inertia.inertia.Inertia._get_page_data
documentation:
Get the data for the page
\:return: A dictionary with the page data

methods.name: inertia.inertia.Inertia._get_flashed_messages
documentation:
Get the flashed messages from the session (pop them from the session)
\:return: List of flashed messages

methods.name: inertia.inertia.Inertia._get_flashed_errors
documentation:
Get the flashed errors from the session (pop them from the session)
\:return: Dict of flashed errors

methods.name: inertia.inertia.Inertia._assert_httpx_is_installed
documentation:
Assert that httpx is installed
\:raises ImportError: If httpx is not installed

methods.name: inertia.inertia.Inertia._set_inertia_files
documentation: Set the Inertia files (CSS and JS) based on the configuration

methods.name: inertia.inertia.Inertia._deep_transform_callables
documentation:
Deeply transform callables in a dictionary, evaluating them if they are callables
If the value is a BaseModel, it will call the model_dump method.
Recursive function

```
param prop

Property to transform

return

Transformed property
```

methods.name: inertia.inertia.Inertia._build_props
documentation:
Build the props for the page.
If the request is a partial render, it will only include the partial keys
\:return: A dictionary with the props

methods.name: inertia.inertia.Inertia._render_ssr
documentation:
Render the page using SSR, calling the Inertia SSR server.
\:return: The HTML response

methods.name: inertia.inertia.Inertia._render_json
documentation:
Render the page using JSON
\:return: The JSON response

methods.name: inertia.inertia.Inertia.share
documentation:
Share props between functions. Useful to share props between dependencies/middlewares and routes
\:param props: Props to share

methods.name: inertia.inertia.Inertia.flash
documentation:
Flash a message to the session
If flash messages are not enabled, it will raise a NotImplementedError
\:param message: message to flash
\:param category: category of the message

methods.name: inertia.inertia.Inertia.location
documentation:
Return a response with a location header.
Useful to redirect to a different page (outside of this server)
\:param url: URL to redirect to
\:return: Response

methods.name: inertia.inertia.Inertia.back
documentation:
Redirect back to the previous page
\:return: RedirectResponse

methods.name: inertia.inertia.Inertia.render
documentation:
Render the page
If the request is an Inertia request, it will return a JSONResponse
If SSR is enabled, it will try to render the page using SSR.
If an error occurs, it will fall back to server-side template rendering
\:param component: The component name to render
\:param props: The props to pass to the component
\:return: InertiaResponse

inner_classes.name: inertia.inertia.Inertia.InertiaFiles
documentation: Helper class to store the CSS and JS file urls for Inertia.js

Inertia class to handle Inertia.js responses
To be used as a dependency in FastAPI
You should use the `inertia_dependency_factory` function to create a dependency, in order
to pass the configuration to the Inertia class
